title: Stelis ornata
intro: Stelis ornata, also known as the ornate Stelis, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis ornata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis ornata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Stelis ornata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Stelis and is further classified under the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is part of the tribe Epidendreae, which is one of the major tribes within the Orchidaceae family. Stelis ornata is native to Central and South America, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species. This classification helps in understanding the plant's characteristics, growth habits, and potential applications.
